summary: A brief New Yorker podcast description highlighting Judith Butler's discussion of their book 'Who's Afraid of Gender?' and the right-wing backlash against gender theory. It briefly touches on Butler's adoption of nonbinary pronouns, which they credit to a younger generation.

principles.statement: Gender is a social construct and a performance.
principles.rationale: Gender is not a fixed, innate characteristic but rather a set of behaviors and roles constructed and enacted within society.

mental_models.name: Gender as Performance
mental_models.description: Viewing gender as a socially constructed performance rather than an innate biological reality.

signature_quotes.text: The young people gave me the ‘they,’... This generation has come along with the idea of being nonbinary. [It] never occurred to me! Then I thought, Of course I am. What else would I be?
signature_quotes.context: Discussing their adoption of nonbinary pronouns.
